ground_truth.title: Fragrance Budget or Limit for 2024?
ground_truth.author: Pippin06
ground_truth.main_content:
Pippin06
Dec 30, 2023

Do you plan on having a monthly fragrance budget or limiting the amount you spend on fragrances in 2024?

My plan is to almost exclusively buy samples or decants, with few exceptions for new bottles to save money as a new retiree in June, while still having fun in this wonderful hobby of ours--thank you StylinLA for the inspiration / idea.

I imagine my estimated budget will be between $300 and $500 max, if any decants turn into bottles.

SmellSharpe
Dec 30, 2023

I will spend a lot less in 2024 than I did in 2022/2023.

I will likely be going to Europe in 2024, so I will very likely buy a few while away. A birthday or Christmas gift here and there as well. Just no impulse buys.

I would hope under $1000 in 2024 spend, but we shall see...

Fiveoaks Bouquet
Dec 30, 2023

Nah, no special budget. It has gotten to the delightful point where I just need to replenish favourites when they run low. In aid of this, there have come to be certain times of the year when special promotions and deals abound and I try to sync buying times with those as much as possible.

Pippin06
Dec 30, 2023

Fiveoaks Bouquet said:
Nah, no special budget. It has gotten to the delightful point where I just need to replenish favourites when they run low. In aid of this, there have come to be certain times of the year when special promotions and deals abound and I try to sync buying times with those as much as possible.

A good place to be.

Fiveoaks Bouquet
Dec 30, 2023

Pippin06 said:
A good place to be.

Took many decades and it kind of snuk (sp?) up on me that that's where it's at, thus the word "delightful!"

Colbourne
Dec 30, 2023

With my collection approaching a bloated and maniacal 1000 (around 980-something now), I will surely be spending a lot less in 2024 and evaluating the treasures I have. I do want to trim the fat as well--there are far too many impulse buys and fragrances I no longer connect with that need better homes. I've also made it certain that March and June will be no-buy months (with future no-buy months coming in more frequency) and I have only 20 left in my want list.

I want to travel more often and have more experiences. Life's too short for me to be bogged down by my material things and when I think about how I have so little time to enjoy what I already possess, it discourages me from collecting. I want to feel satisfied and grateful for the incredible collection that I already own and find more opportunities to "share" it in whatever capacity.

JBHoren
Dec 30, 2023

Fiveoaks Bouquet said:
Nah, no special budget. It has gotten to the delightful point where I just need to replenish favourites when they run low.

I have yet to finish a bottle. I've given-away a few (to good homes), depleted some (as decants; again, to good homes), even tossed a couple(!), but never finished (or broken) any. All of which is to say that "replenishing" is not part of my "fragrance vocabulary". Could that change? Might could... but I doubt it.

Tea_Lilly
Dec 30, 2023

I am making a list of the things that I've wanted for awhile and focus on those. Buy some nicer bottles for my gifts. Less random stuff. I had extra money earmarked for perfume this year that I won't have next year. Just seeing what the budget accomodates.

Pippin06
Dec 30, 2023

Tea_Lilly said:
I am making a list of the things that I've wanted for awhile and focus on those. Buy some nicer bottles for my gifts. Less random stuff.

Hoping you find some bottles you truly love in the year ahead.

Ken_Russell
Dec 30, 2023

Many great replies already, thankful for sharing each of these within this thread.

While seconding the idea of having no specific budget at hand yet and/or also no main ideas for a specific limit, would simply focus on a few personal favorites within the assailable financial frame.

Also scheduling/postponing the rest of scent purchases if/whenever suiting the (pre) established 2024 budget.

Oud Dude
Dec 31, 2023

I didn't spend a penny on fragrances in 2023 and I intend to keep it that way in 2024. This year I really got into downsizing and going deep with the collection that I have. If I did happen to get the itch for something new, I have a trove of samples that I've never touched in the closet.

Varanis Ridari
Dec 31, 2023

I've been buying cheap Atari 2600 games to play on my new Atari 2600+ (Plus).

$4.99 beats $400 or even $40 anyday.

sakecat
Dec 31, 2023

When I restarted the hobby in late 2022 - I focused more on samples, minis, and decants.

I still have a lot to sniff that hasn't been touched yet, so 2024 will be more focused on playing with what I have and downsizing the collection - especially the full bottle and indie oils.

Melbourne2026
Dec 31, 2023

Unless something absolutely mind blowing and boggling to my nostrils is released in 2024 (which is of the utmost unlikely to happen) -

I don't have any plans for anything new to come in, be replaced, or sold.

_Nicolas_
Dec 31, 2023

Only limit will be one bottle a month and trying to keep the size at 50ml or less.

Fiveoaks Bouquet
Dec 31, 2023

Varanis Ridari said:
I've been buying cheap Atari 2600 games to play on my new Atari 2600+ (Plus).
$4.99 beats $400 or even $40 anyday.

Yes, but how do they smell?

Franco65
Dec 31, 2023

I've got bottles and back ups for several lifetimes.. already cutting out on spending. Only hope it will stay so...

Andy the frenchy
Dec 31, 2023

Colbourne said:
I want to feel satisfied and grateful for the incredible collection that I already own and find more opportunities to "share" it in whatever capacity.

I'd gladly volunteer to help you find those opportunities, whenever you need.

Andy the frenchy
Dec 31, 2023

Varanis Ridari said:
I've been buying cheap Atari 2600 games to play on my new Atari 2600+ (Plus).
$4.99 beats $400 or even $40 anyday.

Fiveoaks Bouquet said:
Yes, but how do they smell?

Brought back my ~35yo SNES and Genesis from France this Summer.
Invested another $20 for each for the 'magic' warez super cartridges, and here you go: 1,000 games to keep Andy busy.

A bit plastick-y. But gladly not even a hint of smokiness (so far).

Flakonkrystal
Dec 31, 2023

Wow, what a great post, and impressive planning... In the course of 6 months, I think I acquired about 40 bottles, and probably the equivalent in mini's and samples. (Will go count to see if I am deluding myself!) I do intend to go by one of the few (or only?) dedicated perfume shops in my area, and to purchase some things there, hopefully over the course of months. Ebay will prove to be the true testing ground for me... I probably need to just absolutely stop, since budgeting is not my forte. Maybe I should buy a little more today before 2024 sets in, heh heh... I will be receiving in the mail a large sample coffret from Nishane, and I have a complete set of samples (probably not a good idea) from the Czech 1907 house, which interests me for a couple reasons, and I have sample coffrets from ELDO and from one area of Pierre Guillaume.
Will go count.
P.s. Yes, I was deluding myself. I have about 60 bottles. About 15 are travel-sized.
